verdict: Epistemic foraging (detour to a landmark to relocalise before committing to the goal) EMERGES from a purely pragmatic objective — reach the goal, with no landmark/information-gain/exploration reward anywhere. The proof it is uncertainty-driven and not a hardcoded landmark preference: in a no-drift world the same agent stops detouring. It beats a σ-blind greedy agent and random, collapses when blind to its own uncertainty, and a model-free Q-learner trained only on the goal reward discovers the same policy.
